title: Kattakampal Temple
tagline: Kattakampal Temple — spiritual town of Kerala

overview.short: Kattakampal Temple is located at Kattakampal village in Thrissur district.
overview.description: Kattakampal Temple is located at Kattakampal village in Thrissur district. The main deity of the temple is Shiva in the Sanctum sanctorum facing east. But temple is famous for Kattakampal Bhagavathy in the separate Sanctum sanctorum facing west. It is believed that this temple is one of the 108 Shiva Temples of Kerala and is installed by sage Parasurama dedicated to Shiva.

topPlaces.name: Kattakampal
topPlaces.description: Kattakampal is a village in Thrissur in the state of Kerala, India. It is famous for Pooram which is an annual festival of Kattakampal Temple.

topPlaces.name: Pazhanji
topPlaces.description: Pazhanji is a village with near Kunnamkulam in Thrissur district in the state of Kerala, India. The name "Pazhanji" is derived from the Malayalam word for "old".

topPlaces.name: Pengamuck
topPlaces.description: Pengamuck is a village 32 kilometers from Thrissur in the state of Kerala, India. The word "pengamuck" literally means place of areca nuts, which are grown in the area.

topPlaces.name: Chiravarampathukavu Bhagavathi Temple
topPlaces.description: Chiravarampathukavu Temple enshrines Bhagawati, the mother Goddess, one of the most popular deities in Kerala. The town of Aruvayi, Pazhanji is near the city of Kunnamkulam.

topPlaces.name: Nannamukku
topPlaces.description: Nannamukku is a village in Malappuram district in the state of Kerala, India.

topPlaces.name: Karikkad
topPlaces.description: Karikkad is a village in Thrissur district in the state of Kerala, India.

topPlaces.name: Alamkod, Edappal
topPlaces.description: Alamcode , also Alamkod, is a village in Malappuram district in the state of Kerala, India.

topPlaces.name: Kochanoor
topPlaces.description: Kochanoor is a village in the Thrissur district of Kerala, South India, India. It is situated near the village of Vadakkekad.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Kattakampal Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Kattakampal Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Kattakampal Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Calicut International Airport, Kozhikode (51 km) and the nearest railway station is Ernakulam Junction (Kochi) (85 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Kattakampal Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Kattakampal Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Kattakampal Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Kattakampal Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Kattakampal Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Kattakampal, Pazhanji, Pengamuck, Chiravarampathukavu Bhagavathi Temple, Nannamukku.
